title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 476.1310 - Written requests for the protection of court-related officer's personal information, requirements — list of requests, maintained and distributed — inapplicability, when
text:
476.1310.  Written requests for the protection of court-related officer's personal information, requirements — list of requests, maintained and distributed — inapplicability, when. — 1.  No government agency, person, business, or association shall be found to have violated any provision of sections 476.1300 to 476.1313 if the government agency, person, business, or association has not received a valid written request calling for the protection of the court-related officer's personal information.
  2.  A written request shall be valid if:
  (1)  The court-related officer sends a written request directly to a government agency, person, business, or association; or
  (2)  The court-related officer complies with a Missouri supreme court rule for a state court-related officer to file the written request with the clerk of the Missouri supreme court or the clerk's designee to notify government agencies and such notice is properly delivered by mail or electronic format.
  3.  In each quarter of a calendar year, the clerk of the Missouri supreme court or the clerk's designee shall provide a list of all state court-related officers who have submitted a written request under this section to the appropriate officer with ultimate supervisory authority for a government agency.  The officer shall promptly provide a copy of the list to all government agencies under his or her supervision.  Receipt of the written request list compiled by the clerk of the Missouri supreme court or the clerk's designee by a government agency shall constitute a written request to that government agency for the purposes of sections 476.1300 to 476.1313.
  4.  The chief clerk or circuit clerk of the court where the court-related officer serves may submit a written request on the court-related officer's behalf, provided that the court-related officer gives written consent to the clerk and provided that the clerk agrees to furnish a copy of that consent when a written request is made.  The chief clerk or circuit clerk shall submit the written request as provided by subsection 2 of this section.
  5.  A court-related officer's written request shall specify what personal information shall be maintained as private.  If a court-related officer wishes to identify a secondary residence as a home address, the designation shall be made in the written request.  A court-related officer shall disclose the identity of his or her immediate family and indicate that the personal information of those members of the immediate family shall also be excluded to the extent that it could reasonably be expected to reveal the personal information of the court-related officer.  A court-related officer shall make reasonable efforts to identify specific publicly available content in the possession of a government agency.
  6.  A court-related officer's written request is valid until the court-related officer provides the government agency, person, business, or association with written consent to release the personal information.  A court-related officer's written request expires on such court-related officer's death.
  7.  The provisions of sections 476.1300 to 476.1313 shall not apply to any disclosure of personal information of a court-related officer or a member of a court-related officer's immediate family as required by Article VIII, Section 23 of the Missouri Constitution, sections 105.470 to 105.482, section 105.498, and chapter 130.
(L. 2023 S.B. 103, A.L. 2025 H.B. 145 & 59 merged with S.B. 218)
